premise: My husband’s death was supposed to leave me and his sister equal halves of his $290,000 vintage-car collection and a paid-off lake cabin, but she forged my signature on a transfer, sold the cars for cash and the cabin for $215,000, then told the police I was the thief. I taught myself title fraud examination, proved the notary stamp was a fake, reversed both sales, won a $410,000 civil judgment, bought her house at auction, and turned it into a classic-car museum with her name on a permanent “donor” plaque.
